title: Level 1 — Python Basics
intro:
## What you'll use everywhere

Variables, lists, dicts, loops, conditionals, comprehensions, lambdas, string methods, files. Every ML script chains these.

Edit any cell and hit **Run**. The first run downloads Python (~10MB, one-time, cached for offline).

exercises.title: 1.1 Variables and f-strings
exercises.prompt: Create a variable `model_name` set to `"RandomForest"` and `n_classes` set to `25`. Print both using a single f-string.
exercises.starter:
# Python figures out types automatically
name = "malware_sample"
count = 42
accuracy = 0.95
print(f"Name: {name}, Count: {count}, Accuracy: {accuracy}")

# TODO: model_name = ?
#       n_classes  = ?
#       print f-string with both

exercises.title: 1.2 Lists — index and slice
exercises.prompt: Create `scores = [0.85, 0.90, 0.78, 0.95, 0.88]`. Print the first, the last, and the middle three (indices 1–3 inclusive).
exercises.starter:
attacks = ["dos", "probe", "privilege", "access"]
print(attacks[0])     # first
print(attacks[-1])    # last
print(attacks[1:3])   # slice

# TODO
scores = ...
exercises.hint: Slicing is end-exclusive: a[1:4] gives indices 1,2,3.

exercises.title: 1.3 For loops with enumerate
exercises.prompt: Loop through `scores` and print `"Score i: x"` for each, where i is the index and x is the value.

exercises.title: 1.4 if / elif / else
exercises.prompt: Given a variable `label`, print `"Blocked"` if it's `"spam"`, `"Allowed"` if `"normal"`, otherwise `"Unknown"`. Test with several values.

exercises.title: 1.5 Dictionaries
exercises.prompt: Create `dataset_info = {"name": "NSL-KDD", "samples": 125973, "features": 41}`. Loop through it printing `key -> value` for each entry.

exercises.title: 1.6 Functions
exercises.prompt: Write `is_malicious(label)` that returns `True` if label is not `"normal"`. Call it with both `"dos"` and `"normal"` and print the results.
exercises.solution:
def is_malicious(label):
    return label != "normal"

print(is_malicious("dos"))
print(is_malicious("normal"))

exercises.title: 1.7 List comprehensions
exercises.prompt: Given `labels = ["spam", "normal", "spam", "normal", "spam", "normal"]`, use a comprehension to build `binary_labels` where spam=1, normal=0. Expected: `[1,0,1,0,1,0]`.
exercises.starter:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
doubled = [n * 2 for n in nums]
print(doubled)

labels = ["spam", "normal", "spam", "normal", "spam", "normal"]
# TODO
exercises.solution:
labels = ["spam", "normal", "spam", "normal", "spam", "normal"]
binary = [1 if x == "spam" else 0 for x in labels]
print(binary)
exercises.hint: [expr_if_true if condition else expr_if_false for x in seq]

exercises.title: 1.8 lambda + map
exercises.prompt: Lowercase every string in `raw = ["SPAM", "Normal", "SPAM", "HAM"]` using `map()` and a lambda.
exercises.solution:
raw = ["SPAM", "Normal", "SPAM", "HAM"]
lowered = list(map(lambda x: x.lower(), raw))
print(lowered)

exercises.title: 1.9 String preprocessing chain
exercises.prompt: Given `msg = "  Congratulations! You Won A FREE iPad!  "`, chain methods to: strip, lowercase, split, join with `" "`. Print the result.
exercises.starter:
text = "  FREE Entry To Win A Prize!  "
print(text.strip().lower())

msg = "  Congratulations! You Won A FREE iPad!  "
# TODO
exercises.solution:
msg = "  Congratulations! You Won A FREE iPad!  "
print(" ".join(msg.strip().lower().split()))
exercises.hint: Calling .split() with no args splits on any whitespace.

exercises.title: 1.10 JSON files
exercises.prompt: Build `model_params = {"coef": [0.5, 0.3], "intercept": 0.1}`, dump it to JSON via `json.dumps`, then `json.loads` it back. Print both. (Pyodide's filesystem is virtual — we use string dumps for portability.)
exercises.starter:
import json

model_params = ...
# TODO: dump to a string, load it back, print both
exercises.solution:
import json
model_params = {"coef": [0.5, 0.3], "intercept": 0.1}
blob = json.dumps(model_params)
loaded = json.loads(blob)
print(blob)
print(loaded)
